{"query":"Intel Foundry advanced packaging Amkor partnership E-IB capabilities EMIB-T Foveros-R Foveros-B technical specifications","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1773,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://newsroom.intel.com/intel-foundry/intel-foundry-gathers-customers-partners-outlines-priorities","title":"Intel Foundry Gathers Customers and Partners, Outlines Priorities - Intel Newsroom","content":"SAN JOSE, Calif., April 29, 2025 – Today at Intel Foundry Direct Connect, the company will share progress on multiple generations of its core process and advanced packaging technologies.
The company will also announce new ecosystem programs and partnerships, and welcome industry leaders to discuss how a systems foundry approach enables collaboration with partners and unlocks innovation for customers.
Intel 14A will feature PowerDirect direct contact power delivery, building on the PowerVia backside power delivery technology in Intel 18A.
Because Intel 18A-P will be design rule-compatible with Intel 18A, IP and EDA partners have already started updating their offerings for the variant.
New advanced packaging technology offerings include EMIB-T to enable future high bandwidth memory needs and two new additions to the Foveros architecture: Foveros-R and Foveros-B provide additional efficient and flexible options for customers.
New programs have been added within Intel Foundry’s Accelerator Alliance – Intel Foundry Chiplet Alliance and Value Chain Alliance – along with a range of announcements from top ecosystem partners."},{"url":"https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/foundry/packaging.html","title":"Advanced Packaging Innovations | Chip Packages","content":"2.5D packaging for logic-logic and logic-high bandwidth memory (HBM)."},{"url":"https://amkor.com/blog/amkor-intel-partnership-expands-us-emib-packaging-capacity/","title":"Amkor-Intel Partnership Expands EMIB Packaging Capacity - Amkor Technology","content":"This collaboration aims to enhance the availability of the EMIB technology ecosystem and significantly expand Advanced Packaging capacity in Korea, Portugal and the United States.
The partnership represents a significant milestone in the semiconductor industry’s push to strengthen domestic supply chains for critical packaging technologies.
EMIB technology, developed by Intel, enables high-density interconnects between multiple semiconductor dies in a package, offering performance advantages while minimizing manufacturing complexity compared to traditional silicon interposers.
Through this agreement, Amkor will implement EMIB assembly processes at its Korea, Portugal and upcoming Arizona manufacturing facilities, creating a robust alternative source for this advanced packaging solution."},{"url":"https://www.techpowerup.com/343510/intel-and-amkor-team-up-to-scale-emib-packaging-production","title":"Intel and Amkor Team Up to Scale EMIB Packaging Production | TechPowerUp","content":"Intel is expanding its EMIB packaging capabilities by partnering with long-time OSAT partner Amkor.
While TSMC has been a primary choice for many high-density assemblies, growing interest in Intel's EMIB and Foveros options has led partners like MediaTek, Google, Qualcomm, and Tesla to consider alternatives.

O’Buckley will be joined by executives from MediaTek, Microsoft and Qualcomm.
The Intel Foundry Accelerator Alliance also includes IP Alliance, EDA Alliance, Design Services Alliance, Cloud Alliance and USMAG Alliance.

tavily.raw: {"query":"Intel Foundry advanced packaging Amkor partnership E-IB capabilities EMIB-T Foveros-R Foveros-B technical specifications","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://amkor.com/blog/amkor-intel-partnership-expands-us-emib-packaging-capacity","title":"Amkor-Intel Partnership Expands EMIB Packaging Capacity - Amkor Technology","content":"## Amkor-Intel Partnership Expands EMIB Packaging Capacity. Amkor Technology has entered into a Strategic Partnership with Intel focused on Embedded Multi-Die Interconnect Bridge (EMIB) assembly. This collaboration aims to enhance the availability of the EMIB technology ecosystem and significantly expand Advanced Packaging capacity in Korea, Portugal and the United States. The partnership represents a significant milestone in the semiconductor industry’s push to strengthen domestic supply chains for critical packaging technologies. EMIB technology, developed by Intel, enables high-density interconnects between multiple semiconductor dies in a package, offering performance advantages while minimizing manufacturing complexity compared to traditional silicon interposers. Through this agreement, Amkor will implement EMIB assembly processes at its Korea, Portugal and upcoming Arizona manufacturing facilities, creating a robust alternative source for this advanced packaging solution. Watch Amkor’s COO, Kevin Engel, announce the new partnership at **Intel’s Direct Connect 2025 Keynote**.
